subject: English
title: Prose: When the Trees Walked
summary: In this semi-autobiographical story, Ruskin Bond recounts his childhood in Dehradun with his grandfather, a nature lover who believed trees enjoy human companionship and spend their lives ensuring the survival of the forest.

mcqs.question: Who is the author of the story 'When the Trees Walked'?
Where were the author and Grandfather sitting one morning?
mcqs.answer: They were sitting on the [[veranda steps]].

mcqs.question: What did the author notice moving towards Grandfather?
mcqs.answer: He noticed a [[tendril of a creeping vine]].

mcqs.question: How long did it take for the tendril to touch Grandfather's feet?
Where did Grandfather serve for many years?
mcqs.answer: He served in the [[Indian Forest Service]].

mcqs.question: Where did Grandfather build a bungalow after retirement?
mcqs.answer: He built it on the outskirts of [[Dehradun]].

mcqs.question: Name the fruit trees Grandfather planted.
mcqs.answer: He planted [[lime, mango, orange, and guava]].

mcqs.question: Which valley is known for plants and trees growing tall and strong?
Which tree is described as a 'great show-off'?
Why did Grandmother want to cut down the peepul tree?
mcqs.answer: Because its growth was [[knocking down the bricks]] of the outhouse.

mcqs.question: Why did Grandfather help Grandmother with gardening?
How can one attract butterflies according to Grandfather?
What does the word 'Fertile' mean?
mcqs.answer: It means being able to [[produce a lot of plants]] or crops.

mcqs.question: What does 'Abandoned' mean?
What does 'Vigorous' mean?
Where did Grandfather go to plant trees during the rains?
mcqs.answer: He went into the [[jungle beyond the river-bed]].

mcqs.question: What did Grandfather carry with him to the jungle?
Why did Grandfather plant trees in the forest?
mcqs.answer: For the [[animals and birds]] who need food and shelter.

mcqs.question: List three reasons why men need trees.
mcqs.answer: To [[keep the desert away]], [[attract rain]], and [[prevent soil erosion]].

mcqs.question: What will happen if we don't plant trees ourselves?
mcqs.answer: The world will become [[one great desert]].

mcqs.question: What became a 'nightmare' for the author?
mcqs.answer: The thought of a [[world without trees]].

mcqs.question: Who wrote the poem 'Woodman, spare that tree!'?
mcqs.answer: The poem was written by [[George Morris]].

mcqs.question: What did Grandfather say trees could do in the past?
mcqs.answer: He said they could [[walk about like people]].

mcqs.question: Who cast a spell on the trees to root them in one place?
Which tree manages to move using its aerial roots?
Where did they find a small rocky island?
mcqs.answer: In a [[dry river-bed]] at the foothills.

mcqs.question: Which small tree was already growing on the island?
What saplings did they plant on the island?
mcqs.answer: They planted [[tamarind, laburnum, and coral tree]] saplings.

mcqs.question: What does 'Protested' mean?
What does 'Nightmare' mean?

sections.title: Part 3: The Return and the Miracle

mcqs.question: When was the magic touch of the rains most visible?
Where did plants spring up unexpectedly?
Why did Grandmother want the plants removed from the house?
mcqs.answer: To keep the [[roof from falling down]].

mcqs.question: Where was the author sent during the Second World War?
mcqs.answer: He was sent to a [[boarding school]].

mcqs.question: Where did the grandparents go after selling the house?
In which month did the author return to the river-bed?
What spectacular color caught the author's eye on the island?
mcqs.answer: The [[spectacular red]] blooms of the coral blossom.

mcqs.question: How did the island look when the author returned?
Which birds challenged the author on the island?
What did the trees seem to do when they saw the author?
How had the trees 'walked' again?
What does 'Rambling' mean?
What does 'Beckoned' mean?
When did Ruskin Bond receive the Padma Shri?
When did Ruskin Bond receive the Padma Bhushan?
When do we use 'Who'?
mcqs.answer: Use 'Who' for the [[subject]] of a sentence.

mcqs.question: When do we use 'Whom'?
mcqs.answer: Use 'Whom' for the [[object]] of a sentence.

mcqs.question: How do you choose between Who and Whom?
mcqs.answer: If you can replace it with 'he/she', use [[who]]; if 'him/her', use [[whom]].

mcqs.question: When do we use 'How many'?
When do we use 'How much'?
When is 'Which' used?
mcqs.answer: Used for a [[limited number of choices]].

mcqs.question: When is 'What' used?
mcqs.answer: Used for an [[unlimited number of choices]].

mcqs.question: What does a sentence always begin with?
What is an 'Imperative' sentence?
mcqs.answer: A sentence that gives a [[command or request]].

mcqs.question: What is an 'Exclamatory' sentence?
mcqs.answer: A sentence that expresses [[strong emotion]] or wonder.

mcqs.question: What is an 'Interrogative' sentence?
What is the antonym of 'Weak'?
What is the antonym of 'Rough'?
What is the antonym of 'Dull'?
What is a 'Suffix'?
mcqs.answer: Letters added at the [[end of a word]] to make a new form.

questions.question: Who is the author of 'When the Trees Walked'?
Which tree is described as a 'show-off'?
